comment: At this point, we have not validated the signature. This should be moved underneath the `chain.push_block` call and its error handling when we do other book-keeping like conditionally setting `_production_enabled=true`
msg: At this point, we have not validated the signature. This should be moved underneath the `chain.push_block` call and its error handling when we do other book-keeping like conditionally setting `_production_enabled=true`

comment: I think it is possible that those scraping logs would be using the pattern `COULD NOT FIT, tx:(.*)` to capture the transaction ID. I think it is safer to append this code to the end and label it as an error code. My reasoning being that it is far less likely that `RETRYING ?$` was used in an integrated scraper. Moreso given the inconsistency between a trailing space in the two versions of this message.
msg: I think it is possible that those scraping logs would be using the pattern `COULD NOT FIT, tx:(.*)` to capture the transaction ID. I think it is safer to append this code to the end and label it as an error code. My reasoning being that it is far less likely that `RETRYING ?$` was used in an integrated scraper. Moreso given the inconsistency between a trailing space in the two versions of this message.

comment: Is there a reason we don't just do this in the constructor for kv_db? It is passed cfg.
msg: Is there a reason we don't just do this in the constructor for kv_db? It is passed cfg.

comment: I missed that there was a scope with "initd", so you need to set upper_bound = "inite" (or past the last scope, so you could do param.upper_bound = "z"; which should guarantee that is past the last scope, since I doubt it has any scope values that start with "z".
msg: I missed that there was a scope with "initd", so you need to set upper_bound = "inite" (or past the last scope, so you could do param.upper_bound = "z"; which should guarantee that is past the last scope, since I doubt it has any scope values that start with "z".

comment: - You can copy over the `packed_context_free_data` directly. No need to pass in unpacked and then pack. So you can use the existing constructor. - `bytes(packed_trx)` is not needed, can be just `packed_trx` We should consider removing the partial support for `full` as we don't actually allow a `full` to be used. Note that `get_prunable_size_impl` for `full` asserts. I think we should modify the constructor of `packed_transaction` that takes a `packed_transaction_v0` to assert if `legacy == false`.
msg: - You can copy over the `packed_context_free_data` directly. No need to pass in unpacked and then pack. So you can use the existing constructor. - `bytes(packed_trx)` is not needed, can be just `packed_trx` We should consider removing the partial support for `full` as we don't actually allow a `full` to be used. Note that `get_prunable_size_impl` for `full` asserts. I think we should modify the constructor of `packed_transaction` that takes a `packed_transaction_v0` to assert if `legacy == false`.

comment: Just pass the log_path as a parameter to `block_log_preamble::read_from()` is enough. There's no reason the keep log_path as a member variable.
msg: Just pass the log_path as a parameter to `block_log_preamble::read_from()` is enough. There's no reason the keep log_path as a member variable.

comment: We shouldn't have to go through constructing the key again and retrieving the value, since session_iter points to the key and value already. Should change line769 to: payer_payload pp(*(session_iter).second); const account_name found_payer = pp.payer: and change lines 780-783 to be: primary_iter_store.set_value(store_itr, pp.value, pp.value_size);
msg: We shouldn't have to go through constructing the key again and retrieving the value, since session_iter points to the key and value already. Should change line769 to: payer_payload pp(*(session_iter).second); const account_name found_payer = pp.payer: and change lines 780-783 to be: primary_iter_store.set_value(store_itr, pp.value, pp.value_size);

-   op("amqp-trx-trace-address", bpo::value<std::string>(),

-      "Existing AMQP exchange to send transaction trace messages.");
    op("amqp-trx-trace-reliable-mode", bpo::value<amqp_trace_plugin_impl::reliable_mode>()->default_value(amqp_trace_plugin_impl::reliable_mode::queue),
       "Reliable mode 'exit', exit application on any AMQP publish error.\n"
       "Reliable mode 'queue', queue transaction traces to send to AMQP on connection establishment.\n"
comment: I don't think that these should be options. The trx plugin should _only_ send a trace via `reply-to`, it should _always_ send a trace when a `reply-to` is given, and it should always send the trace to the same AMQP server from which it receives transactions. Broadcasting traces is the responsibility of the trace plugin.
new:
op("amqp-trx-ack-mode", bpo::value<ack_mode>()->default_value(ack_mode::in_block),
       "AMQP ack when 'received' from AMQP, when 'executed', or when 'in_block' is produced that contains trx.\n"
       "Options: received, executed, in_block");
    op("amqp-trx-trace-reliable-mode", bpo::value<amqp_trace_plugin_impl::reliable_mode>()->default_value(amqp_trace_plugin_impl::reliable_mode::queue),
       "Reliable mode 'exit', exit application on any AMQP publish error.\n"
       "Reliable mode 'queue', queue transaction traces to send to AMQP on connection establishment.\n"
msg: I don't think that these should be options. The trx plugin should _only_ send a trace via `reply-to`, it should _always_ send a trace when a `reply-to` is given, and it should always send the trace to the same AMQP server from which it receives transactions. Broadcasting traces is the responsibility of the trace plugin.

comment: The find_plugin is actually rather expensive. This should be setup in plugin init or startup and asserted to be there. Assuming you don't change this to use plugin_interface methods.

"   _port  \tremote port number of peer\n\n"
            "   _lip   \tlocal IP address connected to peer\n\n"
            "   _lport \tlocal port number connected to peer\n\n")
-         ( "p2p-always-resync", bpo::value<bool>()->default_value(false), "Always attempt to resync even when peer sends blocks earlier than this node's last irreversible block")
          ( "p2p-keepalive-interval-ms", bpo::value<int>()->default_value(def_keepalive_interval), "peer heartbeat keepalive message interval in milliseconds")
          ( "p2p-tls-security-group-ca-file", bpo::value<bfs::path>(), "Certificate Authority's certificate file used for verifying peers TLS connection when security groups feature enabled" )
          ( "p2p-tls-own-certificate-file", bpo::value<bfs::path>(), "Certificate file that will be used to authenticate running node if TLS is enabled")
new:
"   _port  	remote port number of peer

"
            "   _lip   	local IP address connected to peer

"
            "   _lport 	local port number connected to peer

")
          ( "p2p-keepalive-interval-ms", bpo::value<int>()->default_value(def_keepalive_interval), "peer heartbeat keepalive message interval in milliseconds")
          ( "p2p-tls-security-group-ca-file", bpo::value<bfs::path>(), "Certificate Authority's certificate file used for verifying peers TLS connection when security groups feature enabled" )
          ( "p2p-tls-own-certificate-file", bpo::value<bfs::path>(), "Certificate file that will be used to authenticate running node if TLS is enabled")
patch:
@@ -3971,6 +3981,7 @@ namespace eosio {
            "   _port  	remote port number of peer

"
            "   _lip   	local IP address connected to peer

"
            "   _lport 	local port number connected to peer

")
+         ( "p2p-always-resync", bpo::value<bool>()->default_value(false), "Always attempt to resync even when peer sends blocks earlier than this node's last irreversible block")
msg: I think we should not add this new option and just make this the behavior.

comment: I'm pretty sure this is not thread safe. You are not allowed to access a socket from more than one thread at a time. Here you have the main thread doing an `async_read` and the internal thread doing an `write`.
msg: I'm pretty sure this is not thread safe. You are not allowed to access a socket from more than one thread at a time. Here you have the main thread doing an `async_read` and the internal thread doing an `write`.

comment: This will cause a dirty db. Seems like we need a way to shutdown without a dirty db.
msg: This will cause a dirty db. Seems like we need a way to shutdown without a dirty db.

comment: Seems like we should at least log that we are ignoring this send.
msg: Seems like we should at least log that we are ignoring this send.

comment: `unlinkable_block_exception` has a very particular meaning. I would not use it. Maybe `eosio::chain::unsupported_feature` or introduce a new one.
msg: `unlinkable_block_exception` has a very particular meaning. I would not use it. Maybe `eosio::chain::unsupported_feature` or introduce a new one.

comment: I'm confused. I thought we were not going to handle the `pause` case for now. If we are going to handle the `pause` case now then I think we should `reject` the all received amqp messages. We would also need to stop consuming from the amqp queue.
msg: I'm confused. I thought we were not going to handle the `pause` case for now. If we are going to handle the `pause` case now then I think we should `reject` the all received amqp messages. We would also need to stop consuming from the amqp queue.

comment: You should be able to detect if the user had invoked the `set code` or `set contract` action and only print out that specific error message in that case. Otherwise this may be confusing if they had not invoked either of those actions. Ideally this should be fixed server side with an appropriate error message, but would also have to involve client side changes if connection is closed (client would probably have to be async).
msg: You should be able to detect if the user had invoked the `set code` or `set contract` action and only print out that specific error message in that case. Otherwise this may be confusing if they had not invoked either of those actions. Ideally this should be fixed server side with an appropriate error message, but would also have to involve client side changes if connection is closed (client would probably have to be async).
